mappings.rationale: Directly covers the art of music, its elements, performance, interpretation, and diverse conceptions — the core subject of this entire section.
Covers music across global cultures, directly relevant to the section's treatment of non-Western musical traditions and genres determined by cultural milieu (section E.4).
Directly covers folk music as a genre determined by cultural milieu, explicitly discussed in sections E.2.b.i and E.4.
Covers blues as a foundational genre related to jazz and popular music, relevant to the section's treatment of jazz (section E.2.b) and popular music (section E.4).
Covers country music as a popular genre with folk roots, relevant to the section's treatment of folk and popular music (section E.4).
Covers early Western music including medieval and Renaissance forms, relevant to the section's treatment of Western music history (section G.1).
Directly covers orchestral performance, instrumentation, and orchestration — topics explicitly addressed in sections B.3, C.7, and E.2.a.
Covers the academic study of music across cultures, directly relevant to the section's treatment of writings about music (section A.5) and non-Western traditions.
Directly covers incidental and background music for motion pictures, explicitly listed under forms of music for the theatre (section E.3.c.iv).
Covers the psychology of musical perception and meaning, directly relevant to the section's treatment of musical meaning (section A.2) and the relation of music to human activities (section A.4).
Directly covers electrophonic instruments, electronic music, recording technology, and the effects of technology on music — topics explicitly addressed in sections B.3.d, E.1.c, and F.
Beethoven is central to the sonata, symphony, and concerto forms explicitly listed in section E.2.a, and to Western music history (section G.1).
Covers the physics of sound including tone, pitch, and timbre — directly relevant to the section's treatment of musical sound (sections B.1 and C).
Covers Romantic music as a major period in Western music history, relevant to the section's treatment of Western music (section G.1).
Covers modernist innovations in music including atonality and new compositional techniques, relevant to Western music history (section G.1).
Sound waves are the physical basis of musical tone and timbre, relevant to the section's treatment of the physical aspects of musical sound (section B.1).
